purpose: Complete forensic surface atlas of GNU Autoconf for 1:1 parity verification against autoconf-rs. This is the canonical reference for every behavioral surface that must be admitted through oracle courts.
doctrine: No surface is claimed until it has a sealed receipt comparing autoconf-rs output byte-for-byte against a pinned GNU Autoconf oracle. This atlas enumerates every surface that requires a receipt.

oracle_versions.key_changes: Current stable release. --help formatting changes, Perl 5.10 requirement, improved Fortran support.
Added AC_PROG_CC_C23, removed deprecated macros. Fixed shell portability issues.
oracle_versions.notes: Previous stable. Should be admitted as secondary oracle for regression testing.

oracle_versions.key_changes: Major release. New AC_CONFIG_MACRO_DIRS, AC_PROG_AR. Dropped Perl 5.6 support. Removed $as_echo backwards compatibility.
oracle_versions.notes: Widely deployed in Linux distributions. Important for compatibility.

oracle_versions.key_changes: Last release before 2.70 rewrite. Very different internals from 2.71+. Uses old M4sh initialization.
oracle_versions.notes: Important for legacy project compatibility. Many projects still use macros that were available in 2.69.

surface_taxonomy.description: Every behavioral surface in GNU Autoconf, organized by category. Each surface requires a separate sealed court receipt.

categories.name: CLI Binary Interfaces
categories.description: Command-line invocation surfaces for all 8 Autoconf binaries.

subsurfaces.surface: Environment variables: AUTOCONF, AUTOM4TE, M4, WARNINGS, PATH
C source scanning for configure.ac hints

categories.name: M4 Macro Engine
categories.description: The GNU m4 engine that processes all Autoconf macros. This is the computational core.

subsurfaces.surface: Quote handling: nesting, changequote, same-pass re-lexing, empty-quote boundary tokens
Comment handling: changecom, dnl, comment consumption
Symbol table: define/undefine/pushdef/popdef, builtin registration, prefix mode
Argument collection: nested parens, quoted commas, whitespace handling, $n/$@/$*/$#
Expansion engine: rescanning, recursion prevention, macro body substitution, pure-text fast path
Diversion system: divert/undivert/divnum, EOF auto-undivert, negative discard, file undivert
Frozen file save/reload: -F/-R, cross-compatibility with GNU m4 frozen format
File inclusion: include/sinclude, -I search path, directory-of-including-file logic
Shell command execution: syscmd/esyscmd/sysval, fd inheritance, signal encoding
Arithmetic: eval (32-bit signed, all GNU operators), incr/decr
POSIX traditional mode (-G): different expansion semantics, no GNU extensions
Syncline output (-s): #line directives for error tracking

categories.name: m4sugar Macro Library
categories.description: Convenience layer above raw M4. Provides structured programming constructs.

subsurfaces.surface: m4_defun/m4_require/m4_provide: macro definition with dependency tracking

categories.name: m4sh Shell-Generation Library
categories.description: Generates portable POSIX shell code from M4 macros.

subsurfaces.surface: M4sh initialization: DUALCASE, ZSH_VERSION, IFS, PATH, as_echo, as_mkdir_p, as_test_x, as_lineno, as_me
Files: AS_MKDIR_P, AS_TMPDIR, AS_BASENAME, AS_DIRNAME, AS_SET_CATFILE, AS_LN_S

categories.name: Core Autoconf Macros
categories.description: The fundamental macros that form the structure of every configure.ac.

subsurfaces.surface: AC_INIT: Package metadata, version, bug-report, tarname, URL. Expands to full prologue.
AC_OUTPUT: Generate config.status and trigger output file creation.
AC_SUBST/AC_SUBST_FILE: Variable substitution in output files.

categories.name: Feature Test Macros
categories.description: Macros that probe the build environment for features.

subsurfaces.surface: AC_EGREP_CPP/AC_EGREP_HEADER: Pattern matching in preprocessor output.

categories.name: Output Generation
categories.description: Configure script and config.status output surfaces.

subsurfaces.surface: Configure script structure: shebang, header, M4sh init, feature tests, config.status generation, footer
config.status file generation: sed substitution, AC_SUBST variable expansion, @VARIABLE@ replacement
config.status header generation: #undef → #define transformation, AH_TEMPLATE expansion
config.status command execution: AC_CONFIG_COMMANDS tag dispatch
Signal trap handling: trap for INT/TERM/HUP in configure and config.status
config.log generation: capturing all command output and exit codes
config.cache: reading/writing cached test results for speed
VPATH support: building in a separate directory from source

perl_to_rust_mapping.description: GNU Autoconf is written in a combination of Perl scripts, shell scripts, and M4 macros. This maps every component to its autoconf-rs Rust equivalent. No GPL code is consulted — this is derived from black-box oracle behavior and public documentation.

components.purpose: Main autoconf driver. Finds autom4te, constructs M4 command line, invokes autom4te with --language=autoconf, handles --trace/--debug/--output/--include.
components.surface_coverage: Invocation model (reads configure.ac, calls M4 engine). Missing: CLI flag parsing, autom4te subprocess, --trace regex matching, environment variable handling.
components.perl_constructs_used: Getopt::Long, File::Basename, File::Spec, strict/warnings, system() calls, pipe opens, signal handlers, tempfile creation, find_via_PATH.
components.rust_alternatives: clap/lexopt for CLI, std::path, std::process::Command, tempfile crate.

components.purpose: Caching M4 wrapper. Manages M4 frozen files for caching, handles --language selection (loads appropriate .m4 libraries), --include path management, --freeze/--reload, --trace output formatting.
components.surface_coverage: None yet. Full autom4te pipeline: M4 invocation, cache management, frozen file I/O, language library loading, trace formatting, autom4te.cfg parsing.
components.perl_constructs_used: Getopt::Long, IO::File, File::stat, Storable (cache serialization), complex M4 argument construction, pipe communication with M4 subprocess.
components.rust_alternatives: serde for cache serialization, std::process for M4 subprocess, std::fs for frozen file I/O.

components.purpose: Generates config.h.in from configure.ac. Scans for AC_DEFINE/AC_DEFINE_UNQUOTED, AH_TEMPLATE, produces template header with #undef lines.
components.surface_coverage: None yet. Must: scan configure.ac for AC_DEFINE/AH_TEMPLATE, generate config.h.in with #undef stubs, handle AH_TOP/AH_BOTTOM.
components.perl_constructs_used: Getopt::Long, autom4te invocation, regex-based macro scanning, text template generation.
components.rust_alternatives: regex crate for scanning, string templating.

components.purpose: Orchestrates autoconf, autoheader, automake, aclocal, libtoolize. Walks directory tree, updates timestamps, handles --force/--install/--symlink.
components.surface_coverage: None yet. Must: directory traversal, subprocess orchestration, dependency tracking, timestamp comparison.
components.perl_constructs_used: Getopt::Long, File::Find, subprocess management, version comparison, --install logic for aux files.

components.purpose: Generates aclocal.m4 by scanning m4/ directories for third-party .m4 macro files. Handles --install to copy system macros locally.
components.perl_constructs_used: Getopt::Long, File::Find, Macro file parsing, --install file copy logic, serial number tracking.

components.purpose: Scans C source files for common Autoconf patterns and generates a configure.scan template.
Updates outdated Autoconf macros in configure.ac. Uses AU_DEFUN mappings to know what to replace.
Extracts #if, #ifdef, #ifndef, #elif, #else, #endif preprocessor condition names from C source files.

Core Perl library: Autom4te::General (utility functions), Autom4te::ChannelDefs (warning channels), Autom4te::Configure_ac (configure.ac parsing), Autom4te::FileUtils (file operations), Autom4te::Getopt (option parsing), Autom4te::Request (autom4te request tracking), Autom4te::XFile (cross-platform file I/O).
components.surface_coverage: configure_ac.rs parses basic AC_INIT. diagnostics.rs has basic Diagnostic types. autom4te.rs is a stub.
components.perl_constructs_used: Perl OOP, complex data structures (hash of arrays), file locking, mktemp, signal handling, die/warn error handling, Carp for backtraces.
components.rust_alternatives: serde for structured data, std::fs for file ops, anyhow/thiserror for error handling.

components.purpose: The computational core: m4sugar provides programming constructs, m4sh generates shell code, autoconf/ provides feature test macros. These are loaded by autom4te at runtime in dependency order.
components.surface_coverage: m4sugar: 18/48 macros. m4sh: 18/42 macros. autoconf_macros: 28/72 macros (general.m4) + 0/320+ across other files.
components.perl_constructs_used: N/A — these are M4, not Perl. But they are loaded by Perl scripts via autom4te.
components.rust_alternatives: Rust structs with byte-level behavioral matching. Macros are built-in, not loaded from files.

ast_parity_bridge.description: The AST parity bridge verifies that autoconf-rs produces token-identical M4 expansion output to GNU Autoconf. This is the foundation of forensic parity.

verification_layers.description: The M4 lexer must produce the same token stream as GNU m4 for identical input.
verification_layers.autoconf_rs_status: partial — basic tokenizer works, but same-pass changequote re-lexing, empty-quote boundaries, and dnl newline consumption need verification.

verification_layers.description: The M4 expansion engine must produce the same output text as GNU m4 for identical macro definitions and invocations.
verification_layers.autoconf_rs_status: partial — basic expansion works, but diversion system, frozen files, include/sinclude, and syscmd/esyscmd not yet implemented.

verification_layers.description: Every m4sugar, m4sh, and Autoconf macro must expand to identical shell script output.
The generated configure script must be byte-identical to the GNU Autoconf oracle output for the same configure.ac input.
verification_layers.autoconf_rs_status: partial — prologue generated but output structure differs significantly from oracle.

verification_layers.description: The generated configure script, when executed, must produce identical config.status, Makefile, and config.h output.
verification_layers.autoconf_rs_status: unimplemented — generated configure not yet executed in sandbox.

code_archaeology.description: Historical context for key behaviors. Understanding WHY Autoconf does things the way it does is essential for correct forensic reproduction.

entries.topic: M4 quote characters: backtick and single-quote
entries.who: Brian Kernighan and Dennis Ritchie (original m4, 1977); GNU m4 maintainers (Francois Pinard, Rene Seindal, Gary V. Vaughan, Eric Blake)
entries.why: Backtick (`) and single-quote (') were chosen because they are visually paired on many terminals and rarely used in natural prose. They are asymmetric opening/closing delimiters, unlike double-quotes which are symmetric.
entries.impact_on_autoconf_rs: Our lexer MUST treat these as single-byte delimiters. Using char-based matching would fail on non-UTF-8 input. Receipt: AC.M4.LEX.1.

entries.who: David MacKenzie (original Autoconf, 1991); Akim Demaille (rewrote AC_REQUIRE with diversions, 2000)
entries.why: Before diversions, AC_REQUIRE used textual ordering — the required macro had to appear textually before the requiring macro. This was fragile. Diversions allow AC_REQUIRE to reorder expansion so dependencies always expand first, regardless of textual position. This is achieved by expanding required macros into a separate diversion that is 'undiverted' at the right time.
entries.impact_on_autoconf_rs: Our RequireTracker must implement diversion-based deferred expansion to correctly handle AC_REQUIRE when the required macro appears textually AFTER the requiring macro.

entries.who: Paul Eggert, Eric Blake, and the Autoconf mailing list community
entries.why: Shell portability is hard. MKS shell requires DUALCASE to handle case-insensitive filesystems. Zsh in sh-compatibility mode still has subtle differences (word splitting, NULLCMD). Each line in the M4sh initialization addresses a real bug found in a real shell on a real platform. The as_echo backslash-testing monstrosity exists because /bin/echo and the shell builtin echo behave differently on different systems, especially with -n and backslash escapes.
entries.impact_on_autoconf_rs: We must emit the M4sh initialization byte-identically to the oracle. The as_echo test with 1024 backslashes is not a joke — it's a necessary evil for portability.

entries.why: Before autom4te, Autoconf ran GNU m4 directly for each configure.ac. This was slow because m4 had to re-expand the entire m4sugar, m4sh, and Autoconf library for every invocation. autom4te solves this by: 1) expanding the standard libraries once and saving the M4 state as a 'frozen file', 2) reloading the frozen file for subsequent invocations, skipping the expensive re-expansion. This reduced configure script generation time by 10-20x.
entries.impact_on_autoconf_rs: We must implement M4 frozen file save/reload cross-compatible with GNU m4 frozen format to achieve comparable performance. Without this, every autoconf-rs invocation will re-expand all standard libraries.

entries.who: David MacKenzie, Ben Elliston, Akim Demaille
entries.why: AC_TRY_RUN compiles and executes a test program. This is impossible when cross-compiling (building for a different target than the host). Autoconf handles this by: 1) detecting cross-compilation mode, 2) providing an 'action-if-cross-compiling' argument to AC_TRY_RUN that supplies a default answer, 3) caching the result in config.cache to avoid re-running. The cross-compilation machinery (--host/--build/--target) is one of the most complex parts of Autoconf.
entries.impact_on_autoconf_rs: We must implement the full cross-compilation detection and AC_TRY_RUN fallback semantics. This requires config.cache support and platform triple handling.

entries.topic: config.status as a separate generated script
entries.why: config.status is generated by configure and executed separately. This two-phase design exists because: 1) configure may be run once but config.status may be re-run multiple times (e.g., after editing config.h), 2) config.status can be distributed with the package and run on the target system without re-running configure, 3) it separates the 'detect' phase (configure) from the 'apply' phase (config.status). This is a key architectural insight.
entries.impact_on_autoconf_rs: Our generated configure scripts must produce a correct, self-contained config.status that can be re-run independently.

entries.topic: m4sugar: from m4 to a programming language
entries.why: Raw M4 is a macro processor, not a programming language. Writing Autoconf macros in raw M4 was error-prone — no data structures, no scoping, no error handling. Akim Demaille built m4sugar as a 'standard library' that provides: structured macro definition (m4_defun vs define), dependency tracking (m4_require/m4_provide), conditionals with proper short-circuit evaluation (m4_if/m4_case vs ifelse), iteration constructs (m4_foreach/m4_for), list/set operations, and diversion management. This transformed Autoconf macro authoring from assembly language to a high-level language.
entries.impact_on_autoconf_rs: Our m4sugar implementation must capture all these semantics, especially the short-circuit evaluation behavior of m4_if (which differs from ifelse) and the diversion-based reordering of m4_require.

entries.why: Autoconf's --help output is not hardcoded. It is dynamically generated from the macros present in configure.ac. AC_ARG_WITH and AC_ARG_ENABLE register their options into a diversion that is collected and formatted at AC_OUTPUT time. AC_PRESERVE_HELP_ORDER (added in 2.69c) was needed because the diversion-based collection destroyed the original ordering of --help entries.
entries.impact_on_autoconf_rs: We must implement diversion-based --help collection and AC_PRESERVE_HELP_ORDER to match the oracle output.
